title: mentzelia lindleyi subsp. crocea
intro: Mentzelia lindleyi subsp. crocea, also known as the yellow blazing star or golden blazing star, is a rare and endemic subspecies of flowering plant in the evening primrose family. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and its name comes from its bright yellow color and star-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of Mentzelia lindleyi subsp. crocea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other upper respiratory tract infections.
